editorial.entity: Star Wars: Visions Presents - The Ninth Jedi

work.title: Star Wars: Visions Presents - The Ninth Jedi

holdReason: accuracy/grounding block: verify-gate CUT: 4 claim(s) not in the gathered sources — Feodor Chin voices Gennoh; Young Mazino voices Nawaam; Chase Sui Wonders voices Tafflah

strengths: The article correctly identifies the authoritative title and release date.
The Key Takeaways are concise and informative.
The structure uses subheads effectively to break up information.
The article avoids describing specific trailer shots or dialogue, adhering to the 'no trailer watch' rule.
The FAQ section adds new, relevant information beyond the main body.
weaknesses: The human voice is largely generic, relying on AI-tell phrases like 'underscores its popularity' and 'promises to delve deeper'.
The phrasing is often stiff and lacks the natural flow of a top news desk, e.g., 'confirming the fan-favorite anime short is expanding into a full series this summer'.
The lead paragraph is a bit dense and could be broken down for better readability.
The article uses 'delve deeper' and 'underscores' which are identified as AI-tells.
The information gain is moderate; while it provides context, the analysis or unique framing is limited.

The 'Sources' section lists Disney+ and StarWars.com, which are not 'authoritative external sources' in the context of reporting on a trailer release; they are primary sources for the content itself. The article relies heavily on FirstShowing.net but doesn't list it as a source.
The article mentions 'Anime Expo 2026' as the trailer debut location, which was not in the provided reference facts and seems like an invention.
